metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the premise: enumerate all five test functions in test_ci_workflow_parity.py and explicitly specify which survive in a new/relocated form after consolidation (the pre-commit SKIP parity tests and the optionality wiring test enforce independent safety properties and must not be silently dropped).
evidence: Plan text: 'tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py exists solely to police that the two copies stay in sync'

tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py lines 191-248: test_branch_sensitive_precommit_hooks_skipped_in_ci and test_ci_redundant_hooks_skipped_in_precommit enforce SKIP env-var parity between the two workflows — a behavioral guard independent of copy-sync

tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py lines 111-142: test_optionality_contract_gates_the_verified_path asserts GERRIT_REFSPEC is passed through to the reusable optionality workflow — another independent wiring guard
ABSENCE: the plan's AC says 'updated or retired coherently' but does not specify which of the five test functions survive in a new form and which truly become redundant after consolidation
findings.finding: The plan asserts test_ci_workflow_parity.py 'exists solely to police that the two copies stay in sync', but a direct read of the file reveals it also enforces three independent behavioral invariants: (a) pre-commit branch-sensitive hook skipping in both workflows (test_branch_sensitive_precommit_hooks_skipped_in_ci), (b) CI-redundant hook de-duplication skipping (test_ci_redundant_hooks_skipped_in_precommit), and (c) optionality reusable-workflow wiring with GERRIT_REFSPEC (test_optionality_contract_gates_the_verified_path). These guards are not merely 'copy-sync policing' — they enforce safety properties that would be silently lost if the file is naively deleted under the 'solely to police sync' premise.
findings.location: Why section: 'tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py exists solely to police that the two copies stay in sync'; AC line 3: 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently'
scenarios: Implementer interprets 'exists solely to police copy sync' as justification to delete test_ci_workflow_parity.py entirely, silently removing the pre-commit SKIP parity guard — a future change could re-introduce the no-commit-to-branch hook without SKIP, causing post-merge branch CI to fail while Verified stays green (the exact bug 'pillared-doddering-fawn' the file was written to prevent).

Implementer deletes the optionality wiring assertion (test_optionality_contract_gates_the_verified_path lines 111-142), losing the guard that gerrit-verify.yaml passes GERRIT_REFSPEC to _optionality.yml — allowing a future refactor to silently revert to branch-head checkout in the Verified lane.
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's Why or AC, replace 'exists solely to police that the two copies stay in sync' with a note that the file contains five test functions, three of which (pre-commit SKIP parity × 2, optionality wiring × 1) enforce behavioral invariants independent of copy-sync and must be explicitly re-expressed — e.g. as assertions on the new reusable workflow's interface — rather than retired.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the structural mismatch is resolved: either (a) define which job(s) the reusable workflow covers (e.g. just the matrix test+gate steps, leaving lint/pip-audit as separate callers-side jobs) or (b) confirm lint/pip-audit are absorbed and explain the test.yml parallelism tradeoff.
evidence: test.yml: lint, pip-audit, test, golden-path, artifact-probe are all separate top-level jobs
gerrit-verify.yaml build-and-test: lint (make lint), pip-audit, and pytest steps are all inlined in the single build-and-test matrix job
Plan states: 'Shared build-and-test steps live in a single reusable workflow_call file' without specifying how the job-boundary difference is resolved
The _optionality.yml precedent does NOT face this mismatch because optionality.yml also has a single optionality job delegating to the reusable workflow
findings.finding: test.yml splits lint and pip-audit into separate top-level parallel jobs, while gerrit-verify.yaml inlines them inside build-and-test. The plan proposes a single _build-and-test.yml reusable workflow but does not address how to reconcile this structural mismatch — naively unifying the step lists either forces test.yml to collapse its job graph or leaves an asymmetric boundary that defeats the consolidation goal.
scenarios: Implementer collapses lint+pip-audit into the reusable workflow, eliminating test.yml's parallelism and slowing branch CI
Implementer keeps lint+pip-audit outside the reusable workflow, leaving them unsynchronized — partial consolidation that still requires parity enforcement for those jobs

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify concretely how `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` should be updated: which file(s) (`_build-and-test.yml`, `gerrit-verify.yaml`, or both) each assertion should scan after extraction, and what structural property (e.g. 'gerrit-verify.yaml invokes ./.github/workflows/_build-and-test.yml') replaces the current inline-text grep.
evidence: _GERRIT_YML = _ROOT / '.github' / 'workflows' / 'gerrit-verify.yaml' — scanned for inline command text at lines 81, 88, 100–103, 118, 199, 228
After extraction, commands like 'make lint', 'pip-audit', 'pytest -m integration', 'pre-commit run --all-files' will live in _build-and-test.yml, not gerrit-verify.yaml
ABSENCE of any plan statement about which file(s) the updated parity test should scan
findings.finding: test_ci_workflow_parity.py hard-codes scans of gerrit-verify.yaml's raw text for all gate command signatures (_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES, scripts regex, optionality wiring, pre-commit SKIP checks). After build-and-test is extracted into _build-and-test.yml, every assertion that reads _GERRIT_YML for those command strings will fail (the commands will be in the reusable file, not inline). The plan says 'updated or retired coherently' but does not specify what the updated test should verify nor which file(s) it should scan.
findings.location: AC line 3: 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently'; tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py lines 81, 88, 100, 103, 118, 199, 228
scenarios: Developer creates _build-and-test.yml, removes inline steps from gerrit-verify.yaml, runs 'make lint + tests' — test_ci_workflow_parity.py assertions against _GERRIT_YML fail immediately, blocking the change from landing through Gerrit.
If the parity test is simply retired without replacement, the safety property it provides (drift detection between the two CI legs) is silently lost.
findings.suggested_fix: Update _GERRIT_YML assertions to verify that gerrit-verify.yaml *calls* ./.github/workflows/_build-and-test.yml (structural wiring check), and redirect the gate-command-signature assertions (_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES, scripts regex, pre-commit SKIP) to scan _build-and-test.yml instead of _GERRIT_YML.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the `vote` job and `im-open/workflow-conclusion` aggregation as explicit consumers to update/verify when `build-and-test` becomes a reusable workflow call, and confirm GitHub's job-conclusion rollup for reusable workflows covers matrix failures correctly.
evidence: needs: [clear-vote, require-ticket, build-and-test, optionality, golden-path, verify-identity] at gerrit-verify.yaml:521
im-open/workflow-conclusion aggregates run conclusion from all jobs via REST; reusable-workflow jobs appear as child jobs, not as the calling job — may affect WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION correctness
ABSENCE of any plan mention of the vote job's needs list or the workflow-conclusion aggregation changing
findings.finding: The `vote` job in gerrit-verify.yaml has `needs: [clear-vote, require-ticket, build-and-test, optionality, golden-path, verify-identity]`. When `build-and-test` becomes a workflow_call invocation of `_build-and-test.yml`, the `needs` reference and the im-open/workflow-conclusion job-list aggregation must still resolve correctly. The plan does not enumerate the `vote` job as a consumer requiring update.
findings.location: gerrit-verify.yaml line 521; plan 'Why' section describing the 3-job shape
scenarios: If the build-and-test job in gerrit-verify.yaml is replaced by a `uses:` call, its child jobs (from the reusable) are separate job entries; the `vote` job's `needs: build-and-test` still resolves to the calling job's conclusion, which may be 'success' even if inner matrix cells fail, depending on how GitHub rolls up reusable workflow conclusions.
workflow-conclusion REST scan may or may not see the reusable's inner jobs depending on the GitHub API's job-listing behavior for nested reusable calls.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC3 what structural or unit-level test (e.g. a YAML-parse/schema assertion or updated parity test) validates that each caller passes the correct `gerrit-refspec` parameter to `_build-and-test.yml`, so parameter-wiring bugs are caught without a live dispatch.
evidence: 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently with the new structure' — 'coherently' is underspecified
'checkout parameterized by gerrit-refspec (branch-head for the mirror lane, exact patchset for the Verified gate)' in Why section — this parameterization has no named test coverage
The parity test's stated purpose was to police drift between two copies; its replacement by shared code prevents content-drift but not invocation-parameter-drift
findings.finding: AC3 says test_ci_workflow_parity.py will be 'updated or retired coherently' but does not specify what new structural coverage (if any) validates that both callers wire the reusable workflow's parameters correctly — in particular that gerrit-refspec is threaded through properly in each invocation. If retired without replacement, parameter-wiring bugs (e.g. wrong ref passed to _build-and-test.yml) would only be caught by the live Verified-vote dispatch, not by any fast-feedback unit/structural test.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently with the new structure'
scenarios: _build-and-test.yml is created correctly but gerrit-verify.yaml passes the wrong ref (e.g. HEAD instead of the patchset SHA); parity test is retired; the only catch is the live Verified dispatch, which may not be run in every PR cycle
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'If test_ci_workflow_parity.py is retired, add a replacement structural test (e.g. assert both callers pass `gerrit-refspec` and that the value differs between test.yml branch-head and gerrit-verify.yaml patchset invocations) to preserve fast-feedback coverage of parameter wiring.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what the updated/replacement parity test enforces after consolidation — e.g., assert both callers invoke `_build-and-test.yml`, assert caller-side jobs (lint, golden-path, pip-audit) are present in both workflows, and assert no scripts/*.py gate exists in test.yml that is absent from both gerrit-verify.yaml and the reusable workflow.
evidence: test_ci_workflow_parity.py: 'These tests fail the build the moment the two workflows diverge, so drift is caught at review time instead of on main.'
test_ci_workflow_parity.py test_no_drift_script_gate_is_verified_only_in_branch_ci: auto-catches new scripts/*.py gates in test.yml absent from gerrit-verify.yaml
AC3: 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently with the new structure' — no specification of replacement invariant

Existing comment in test_ci_workflow_parity.py: 'a gate present only in test.yml can only fail post-merge, letting a red change land with a green Verified vote (this is exactly how a stale docs/env-vars.md reached main)'
findings.finding: AC3 says test_ci_workflow_parity.py will be 'updated or retired coherently' but does not specify what structural invariant replaces its safety net. The parity test currently auto-detects new gates added to test.yml that are missing from gerrit-verify.yaml — a historically-triggered protection. After consolidation, gates inside the reusable file are automatically shared, but gates outside it (golden-path, lint, pip-audit, require-ticket, verify-identity if they remain caller-side) still need drift protection, and new risks emerge (e.g. a caller omitting a required `with:` input, matrix mismatch). Retiring the test without specifying the replacement leaves the system with a net reduction in observable correctness enforcement.
scenarios: A future gate is added to test.yml but not to _build-and-test.yml; the retired parity test would have caught this, but now nothing does
A caller omits a required gate step that lives outside the reusable workflow (e.g. golden-path); no test detects the gap

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC2) that explicitly verifies the reusable workflow receives and uses the correct checkout ref for each caller — branch-head for test.yml and exact patchset SHA for gerrit-verify.yaml.
evidence: 'checkout parameterized by gerrit-refspec (branch-head for the mirror lane, exact patchset for the Verified gate)' — Why section
AC1 only requires the file exists and is invoked by both callers; it does not verify the refspec parameter is correctly threaded through each call site
findings.finding: The plan describes parameterizing checkout by gerrit-refspec ('branch-head for the mirror lane, exact patchset for the Verified gate') as a key deliverable, but no AC explicitly verifies that this parameterization is correct for both invocation paths.
findings.location: Why section ('checkout parameterized by gerrit-refspec') vs. AC1 ('invoked by both test.yml … and gerrit-verify.yaml')
scenarios: gerrit-verify.yaml invokes the reusable workflow but passes the wrong ref (e.g., branch head instead of patchset SHA), silently testing the wrong commit and still casting Verified +1
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2 to: 'The Verified vote still works end-to-end, including confirmation that gerrit-verify.yaml passes the patchset refspec (not branch head) to the reusable workflow, verified by inspecting the workflow_call inputs in a real dispatch log.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'e.g. _build-and-test.yml' with the committed canonical filename (e.g. state 'named _build-and-test.yml') so the executor has a definite target.
evidence: 'Shared build-and-test steps live in a single reusable workflow_call file (e.g. _build-and-test.yml)'
ABSENCE: no canonical filename is committed to; 'e.g.' defers the choice to the executor.
findings.finding: The reusable workflow filename is given only as 'e.g. _build-and-test.yml', leaving the actual filename as an executor decision rather than a stated default.
scenarios: Executor picks a different name, leading to inconsistency with the parity-test update or reviewer expectations.
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC1 to: '...live in .github/workflows/_build-and-test.yml, invoked by both test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan to the Risk section — e.g., 'revert the _build-and-test.yml introduction commit and restore the inline job in gerrit-verify.yaml' — so the team can restore Verified voting quickly if the reusable workflow is defective.
evidence: 'gerrit-verify.yaml is the SOLE Verified-vote gate — high blast radius' — Risk section
'Replacing the parity test removes a safety property (two independent copies)' — Risk section
No rollback path, revert procedure, or expand-contract sequencing described anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges gerrit-verify.yaml is 'the SOLE Verified-vote gate — high blast radius' and that replacing the parity test 'removes a safety property,' but provides no explicit rollback or expand-contract plan — acknowledgement alone does not satisfy T4.
findings.location: Risk section ('high blast radius. Must be its own independently-reviewed Gerrit change')
scenarios: The reusable workflow has a subtle bug; both test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml break simultaneously; there is no documented procedure to restore the prior two-file structure quickly.
Parity test is retired and the reusable workflow is later modified incompatibly; with no safety net and no rollback plan, the breakage goes undetected until a Verified vote fails in production.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Risk section: 'Back-out plan: if the reusable workflow is defective post-merge, revert the Gerrit change that introduced _build-and-test.yml and restore the inline build-and-test job in gerrit-verify.yaml from the prior commit. Keep the old job definition in a comment or branch until AC2 is confirmed in a real gerrit-verify dispatch.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Record in the plan that the reusable-workflow approach has been approved (e.g. link an ADR, a Gerrit +2, or a team decision note) so the pre-condition is resolved and no longer a TBD.
evidence: 'Replacing the parity test removes a safety property (two independent copies); confirm the reusable-workflow approach is acceptable before implementing.'
ABSENCE: no recorded confirmation, ADR reference, or decision note appears in the plan.
findings.finding: The body says 'confirm the reusable-workflow approach is acceptable before implementing' but records no resolution of that confirmation, leaving it as an open TBD pre-condition that could block an executor.
findings.location: Why / Risk section: 'confirm the reusable-workflow approach is acceptable before implementing'
scenarios: An executing agent reads this as a blocking prerequisite and stops to ask whether the approach is approved before writing any code.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line note such as 'Decision: reusable-workflow approach approved in [ADR-XXXX / team discussion on DATE].' to close the open question.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving step for AC2, e.g.: 'Push a test patchset through Gerrit; confirm the gerrit-verify CI job (invoking _build-and-test.yml) completes and the change shows Verified +1 in the Gerrit UI / via `ssh gerrit gerrit query --format=JSON change:<id> | jq .currentPatchSet.approvals`.'
evidence: AC2 states the outcome ('casts Verified +1') but provides no step mapping it — no command, no UI path, no log artifact.
ABSENCE: no 'how to verify' step is described anywhere in the plan for this criterion.
findings.finding: AC2 ('Verified vote still works end-to-end') has no proving command or step describing HOW to trigger the real gerrit-verify dispatch and what observable artifact to inspect to confirm Verified +1 was cast.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2: 'The Verified vote still works end-to-end (a real gerrit-verify dispatch casts Verified +1).'
scenarios: Executor submits the Gerrit change but has no documented way to confirm the Verified vote was cast by the new reusable-workflow path rather than a cached/old run; completion evidence is ambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'coherently' with a concrete observable outcome, e.g.: 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py is retired (deleted) because parity is enforced structurally, OR updated to validate the new reusable-workflow invocation; make test passes either way.'
evidence: 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently with the new structure'
ABSENCE: no definition of what 'coherently' requires — e.g., no dangling assertions, no orphaned test file, CI passes — is given.
findings.finding: AC3 uses the subjective qualifier 'coherently' ('test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently') without defining what coherent means operationally, making the criterion unevaluable against a clear standard.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3: 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently with the new structure.'
scenarios: Executor retires the file but leaves a broken import; or updates it to assert on the old structure; reviewer and executor disagree on whether the result is 'coherent'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or AGENTS.md section documenting the decision to consolidate CI steps into a reusable workflow_call, explicitly recording what replaces the parity-test safety guarantee (e.g., 'single source of truth enforced by shared file, rather than two-copy sync policed by a test').
evidence: Plan states 'Replacing the parity test removes a safety property (two independent copies)' but only requires the file be 'retired coherently', with no requirement to document WHY the old safety approach was replaced and what now substitutes for it.
No ADR, AGENTS.md update, or design-doc update is mentioned anywhere in the plan.
A future maintainer who encounters the retired parity test will have no recorded rationale for the architectural choice.
findings.finding: The plan removes an explicit safety mechanism (test_ci_workflow_parity.py) and introduces a new architectural pattern (shared reusable workflow for the Verified-vote gate) but does not require capturing this decision and its rationale in an ADR, AGENTS.md, or design-doc update.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'test_ci_workflow_parity.py updated or retired coherently with the new structure'; Risk section — 'Replacing the parity test removes a safety property'
scenarios: A future contributor re-introduces workflow duplication, unaware that the reusable-workflow contract is now the enforced invariant.
Onboarding a new CI maintainer who wonders why the parity test was removed and what the current safety guarantee is.
findings.suggested_fix: Create docs/adr/NNNN-reusable-ci-workflow.md (or a section in AGENTS.md under 'CI Architecture') covering: (1) the old pattern and why it was fragile, (2) the new _build-and-test.yml contract, (3) what invariant replaces the parity test (single canonical file), and (4) the blast-radius risk and how it was mitigated (independent Gerrit change + end-to-end Verified-vote smoke test).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm and document how the `needs: [clear-vote, require-ticket]` dependency chain is preserved when `build-and-test` is extracted to a reusable workflow (the `_optionality.yml` analogy doesn't cover this — a wrapper job in the Gerrit caller is likely required; call it out explicitly in the plan).
evidence: gerrit-verify.yaml line 117: `needs: [clear-vote, require-ticket]` on the `build-and-test` job
_optionality.yml has no `needs:` — invoked directly from optionality.yml (no wrapper job needed)
Plan states 'the pattern _optionality.yml already uses' — implying a direct port — but the build-and-test job's DAG position is not analogous to the optionality case
ABSENCE: the plan contains no mention of the `needs:` dependency chain or the wrapper-job pattern required to preserve it
findings.finding: The plan analogises the proposed `_build-and-test.yml` reusable workflow directly to `_optionality.yml`, but the two cases differ in a load-bearing way: `_optionality.yml` has no upstream `needs:` dependencies in either caller, whereas `gerrit-verify.yaml`'s `build-and-test` job sits behind `needs: [clear-vote, require-ticket]`. A `workflow_call` job cannot inherit `needs:` from inside the callee — the Gerrit caller must wrap the reusable call in a job that declares those `needs:`. The plan does not acknowledge this wrapper requirement, so an implementer following it naively will either (a) lose the `clear-vote`/`require-ticket` ordering guarantee or (b) discover the structural complexity mid-implementation. This is premature abstraction if the structural fit is not confirmed before work starts.
findings.location: Why section / AC line 1 ('invoked by both test.yml (branch head) and gerrit-verify.yaml (patchset)')
scenarios: Implementer wraps the reusable call in a job with `needs: [clear-vote, require-ticket]` — correct but adds an extra indirection layer the plan doesn't budget for.
Implementer ports naively (no wrapper), causing `build-and-test` to race with `require-ticket`, allowing a commit without a valid ticket to pass the Verified gate.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
